name: Programmable Rendering Pipeline

userStory.iWant: to intercept and modify the rendering process at different stages
userStory.soThat: I can build custom layout engines, global post-processing effects (like CRT scanlines or bloom), and custom diffing strategies.

acceptanceCriteria: The TEA view() function no longer immediately writes to the TTY
A standard RenderPipeline is established with defined stages (e.g. Layout, Paint, PostProcess, Diff, Output)
Users can register custom middleware functions for each stage of the pipeline
The PostProcess stage receives the fully composited Surface before diffing occurs
backwardCompatibility: The default pipeline will emulate the hardcoded v3.0 double-buffer logic. Existing apps will not need to know the pipeline exists.

inScope: Middleware registry in the Runtime configuration
Surface-to-Surface filter primitives for the PostProcess stage

testPlan.goldenPath: Register a 'Grayscale' post-processing middleware. Run the app and assert that the final TTY output contains no colored SGR escape codes, only luminance values.
edgeCases: Middleware that alters the dimensions of the Surface (should trigger a safe re-layout or clip)
Multiple heavy middleware functions causing frame drops (needs performance profiling)
Middleware throwing an error during the render cycle (should fail gracefully without crashing the app)
testPlan.fuzzStress: Run 10 different post-processing filters simultaneously on a 200x60 grid and ensure the frame rate stays above 30fps.
failureCases: Infinite loops in the pipeline logic

estimatedComplexity.timeComplexity: O(S * M) where S is surface size and M is number of middleware
